feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: RISE Private Cloud – On-prem subnet unreachable after ISP failover
entry.content:
We are running SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud (our Hyperscaler is AWS) connected to on-premise via Direct Connect with dual ISP backup.

During primary ISP failure, connectivity to a specific on-premise subnet is lost while other subnets remain reachable.

This suggests a potential routing asymmetry or selective route propagation issue during failover.

We would like to validate routing symmetry and reachability from the SAP cloud side.

Questions:

  • Is it possible to execute traceroute or TCP connectivity tests from SAP managed application servers in AWS environments?
  • Is this typically handled by SAP Cloud Operations via incident ticket?
  • Are there recommended validation procedures from SAP side during customer ISP failover scenarios?

entry.title: CDS-Based OData 2.0 – Power BI Service Returns Status Code 400 (Invalid Credentials) for Production
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are experiencing an issue with a CDS-based OData service (auto-published via @odata.publish: true) when connecting from Power BI Service.

System & Technical Info:

  • OData Version: 2.0

  • Authentication Type: Basic Authentication

  • Same User ID used in both clients

  • User authorizations confirmed identical (Full Access)

Issue Details:

  • Client 300 (Production) – Power BI Service: Fails with “Failed to update data source credentials: The credentials provided for the OData source are invalid. Status Code: 400.”

  • Client 300 – Power BI Desktop: Works without issues

  • Client 180 (Development) – Power BI Service & Desktop: Works without issues

  • OData URLs (Client 180 & 300): Both accessible and responsive

Checks Performed:

  • No authorization errors in SU53

  • No relevant errors in /IWFND/ERROR_LOG

  • Roles and authorizations between clients verified identical

Question:
Has anyone encountered a situation where a CDS-based OData service works in Power BI Desktop, works in Power BI Service for one client, but fails in Power BI Service for another client with Status Code 400?

Which additional checks would you recommend for:

  • CDS OData activation consistency

  • /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE registration

  • SICF node configuration differences

  • Client-specific security or profile parameters

  • Basic Authentication handling in Power BI Service

Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Where can I download SAP GUI?
entry.content:
Hello, we cannot find the SAP GUI in the catalog https://me.sap.com/softwarecenter  please give me the right link where we can download it.

Thanks

entry.title: KSEF QR Code Generation
entry.content:
Hi 

I have a question regarding implementing the KSEF QR code on Invoices . 

I know the QR code is made of a structure of : 

 

 

>Invoice Number : Eg : 0913502961

> Invoice date: Eg : 2025-11-28
>Seller NIP: Eg : 5252987654
>Invoice hash (SHA-256): abc123xyz456...(Base64URL encoded) Eg : UtQp9Gpc51y-u3xApZjIjgkpZ01js-J8KflSPW8WzIE

Most of the data above we know how to get , however we have a problem generating and retriving the Invoice hash (SHA-256). 

Can someone help point us in the right direction as to how to get this data from and how its computed . 

Any help is appreciated 

Thanks 

RK

entry.title: WHT (Withholding Tax) input when create PR (Purchase Requisition) or PO (Purchase Order)
entry.content:
Hi All FI Experts.

If we may know,
currently there is an inquiry from the end-user about the feasibility on setting-up or configuring the automatic calculation of WHT (Withholding Tax) based amount at document line-item level when parking/posting invoice using transaction code MIR7 or MIRO; by firstly having WHT Tax and WHT Code information at PO Item level since PR (Purchase Requisition)/PO (Purchase Order) creation using transaction code ME51N or ME21N.

Would you like to kindly help us on giving advice whether there is any particular technical ABAP coding using BAPI / BAdI, or relevant statement arranged in Validation GGB0, or another way needed for this requirement, please? 

Thank you so much, and we are looking forward to discussing this with you.

Ani.

 

cc @raghavendra_praveen @Priya2310 @Divyesh_Mistry  @former_member682878  @former_member183424  @JL23  @s_raghavan @former_member466170 @akshyadhingra @anss_shahid 

MM (Materials Management) FIN (Finance) MM Purchasing MM Invoice Verification SAP Invoice and Goods Receipt Reconciliation  SAP S/4HANA Finance FIN Posting and Taxes

entry.title: Transferring Work Center Hierarchy to SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Wanted to know whether work center Hierarchy created via CR21 in S/4 can be transferred to SAP Digital Manufacturing.

 

Regards,

Rahaman

entry.title: Question:Whether the NetWeaver SDK can be used with S/4HANA 2025 and its support expiration date
entry.content:
Is it possible to communicate with SAP S/4HANA 2025 (on-premises/private cloud) using the NetWeaver RFC SDK?

Additionally, while the end of support for the NetWeaver RFC SDK has been announced for the end of 2027, is there a successor product available after this date?

entry.title: Error calling method "cl_apj_rt_api=>schedule_job" from RAP App
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I've been dealing with an issue when calling method "cl_apj_rt_api=>schedule_job" from the behavior implementation class of a custom RAP App.

The requirement is as follows:

  • The user will select a register from the report.
  • Based on the selected register, the program should print (in a physical printer) a PDF generated from an Adobe Forms. 

To do this, I've added an action in the Behavior Definition. This action (named 'Print') is called when the user selects a register and clicks a custom button.

In the implementation of action 'Print', we're trying to create a Job that will handle the calls to the necessary functions to generate and print the PDF.

METHODS print FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION label~print.

METHOD print.
lv_job_template_name = 'ZJT_MYJOB'.
ls_job_start_info-start_immediately = abap_true.
cl_apj_rt_api=>schedule_job(  EXPORTING    iv_job_template_name = lv_job_template_name
                                                     iv_job_text = 'SPOOL Registration Job'
                                                     is_start_info = ls_job_start_info ).
ENDMETHOD.

To create the Job, we've already created an Application Job Template, the corresponding Application Job Catalog Entry, and the implementing class.

However, when the app is executed, we are getting an error at the time method "schedule_job" is executed:

 I understand that the 'Insert' statement is causing the issue, but is there any way to work around this error?

entry.title: How to pass longtext via extraction enable CDS to Datasphere
entry.content:
I need to pass longtext in datasphere using extraction enabled CDS. However, neither longtext nor table functions can be used for it.
Could you please to identify the best way to do it.

Regards,
Malika Bathla.

SAP Datasphere SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Updating sales order user status via Patch operation
entry.content:
Hello, 

we are trying to update Sales order User status on header level in the context of Sales order CRUD API - API_SALES_ORDER_SRV through Patch operation. However, we are not able to do so. Tried to update through Property "StatusCode", but unfortunately the response is giving Property invalid even though this Property can be found in the metadata of the API. We are trying with the following batch request:

--batch
Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=changeset

--changeset
Content-Type: application/http
Content-Transfer-Encoding: binary
Content-ID: 1

PATCH A_SalesOrder(SalesOrder='XXXXXXXXXX') HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
If-Match: *
Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8

{"StatusCode":"E0029"}


--changeset--
--batch--

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: what are the alternative options for VBS scripts since it will be deprecated by Microsoft?
entry.content:
I'm contacting you to confirm the future direction of SAP Scripting (VBS-based), an automation feature for SAP.

As Microsoft has indicated its intention to gradually deprecate VBS,

I would like to confirm whether SAP Scripting can continue to be used, or whether it is scheduled for deprecation.

If it is going to be deprecated, I would also appreciate it if you could also let me know:

- Are there any alternative automation methods (officially recommended tools)?

- If so, what are their overviews?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Clean Core: architecture challenge or decision-making challenge?
entry.content:
Hi everyone! We recently wrote a longer post for the SAP Community blog we argue that the biggest challenge behind Clean Care is organizational rather than architectural.

In many projects we've seen, the pressure to customize usually reflects problems in terms of decision-making, lack of governance, or even some difficulty to align stakeholders around standard processes.

We'd be really interested in hearing how others have experienced this issue that is more human than technical. Which was the hardest part of dealing with Clean Core in your projects? Were the issues -if there were any- technical or more about organizational agreement?

entry.title: What’s one SAP feature you wish more customers used?
entry.content:
Hello everyone! 

In a recent project we realized we were barely using some capabilities that were already available in the system, which made me curious.

Is there any SAP feature that you think customers are not using enough (or forgetting completely) in real projects? Something that could actually make a difference but almost never gets adopted.

entry.title: Workaround for Multiple Tiles per App Descriptor Item (URL/GUI Apps) in FLPAM – Is this best practic
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I recently ran into an architectural limitation in the Fiori Launchpad App Manager (/UI2/FLPAM) and wanted to share my workaround but also ask seasoned Fiori developers if this approach might have any hidden pitfalls.

The Scenario: I have a custom URL-based application. I need to create about 50 different tiles for this single URL, where the only difference between the tiles is the tile title and the parameters passed in the URL.

The Problem: Initially, I created one custom App Descriptor Item (Type: URL App) in my Technical Catalog, defined the Target Mapping, and tried to add multiple tiles under the "Tiles" tab. However, the "Add Tile" button was greyed out. I tested this with Transactional (GUI) apps as well, and saw the exact same behavior.

After digging into the SAP documentation, I found this specific note:

"Mapping multiple tiles to a launchpad app descriptor item is only supported for the following app types: SAPUI5 Fiori App, SAPUI5 Fiori App on SAP Cloud Platform."

Because of this restriction, FLPAM forces a strict 1:1 relationship between the Target Mapping and the Tile for URL and Transactional apps.

The Workaround: To avoid flooding my Business Catalogs with redundant Target Mappings, I used the following approach:

  1. In the Technical Catalog (TC): I duplicated the App Descriptor Item 50 times. This resulted in 50 Tiles and 50 identical Target Mappings (same Semantic Object and Action), with the only differences being the parameters and titles defined on the Tiles themselves.
  2. In the Business Catalog (BC): Instead of pulling in all 50 App Descriptor Items (which would map 50 TMs and 50 Tiles), I mapped all 50 Tiles, but only referenced one single Target Mapping.
  3. Target Mapping Configuration: I ensured the single Target Mapping allows additional parameters so it can dynamically accept the parameters passed from whichever of the 50 tiles the user clicks.

Because Fiori Intent-Based Navigation (IBN) resolves at runtime by looking for a matching Semantic Object/Action in the user's assigned Business Catalogs, the launchpad successfully routes all 50 tiles through that single Target Mapping and passes the correct tile-specific parameters.

My Questions for the Fiori Experts: While this reverse-engineers the flexibility we used to have in the old Launchpad Designer (/UI2/FLPD_C), I want to make sure I'm not setting a trap for myself down the road:

  1. Are there any hidden issues with having 50 App Descriptor Items in a Technical Catalog where 49 of their Target Mappings are essentially "orphaned" (never mapped to a Business Catalog)?
  2. Will this approach cause any unforeseen issues with Fiori Search, caching, or future platform upgrades?
  3. Is there a better/standard way to handle 1:N (Target Mapping to Tiles) relationships for URL/GUI apps in the modern App Manager (/UI2/FLPAM) without reverting to the deprecated FLPD?

Thanks in advance for your insights!

entry.title: Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty - SAP S4 to Biztalk 2016
entry.content:
Hello Team,

While Connecting from SAP S4 Cloud to MS Biztalk 2016 it is failing with below error. Totally stucked up with this error for few weeks. SAP states to uplift SAP NCO connector to 3.1.6 it doesn't really help.

 

Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty - Stucked in SM58

Latest Update from SAP- Really issue is strange and fishy!!!

Logs:

 

MS REPLY:

 

 

 

 

Thanks and Regards,

Rajesh PS

entry.title: How to create WTs in EWM without a batch?
entry.content:
Hello,

We work with a Storage Type that stores multiple batches in the same storage bin.

When creating the picking WTs, system determines a specific batch to be created.
However, in this multi batch ST, warehouse workers would take the lot that is closest to them, to avoid downtime from having to move all the pallets to look for a particular lot that is filled in the WT.

So, how can we create picking WTs without a predetermined batch?

If you can make a recommendation to improve this in operation, it is also welcome.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: STO Movement between Storage Locations
entry.content:
While working with SAP Transportation Management (SAP TM), one interesting design consideration arises when transportation planning is required between storage locations within the same plant.

Since the planning engine in SAP TM relies heavily on location modeling, it is important that the system can clearly differentiate the source and destination. One effective approach is to model Plant–Storage Location combinations as separate TM locations, allowing the system to recognize an actual transportation movement.

In some cases, a custom enhancement may also be required to ensure that both the source and destination locations in Freight Units or Freight Orders are derived correctly based on the storage location information.

This highlights how master data modeling and small design decisions can significantly influence transportation planning behavior in SAP TM.

Always interesting to see how thoughtful design can simplify complex logistics scenarios.

#SAP #SAPTM #SAPCommunity #SupplyChain #Logistics

entry.title: Restriction on GL Account and Cost Center in Manage Service Entry Sheet App
entry.content:
Restriction on GL Account and Cost Center in Manage Service Entry Sheet App

Hello Experts,

We are trying to enforce restrictions on GL Account and Cost Center combinations in the Fiori Manage Service Entry Sheet (SES) app so that invalid combinations are blocked before the SES can be saved.

However, we observed that in the current SES processing flow, validation logic is not always executed before the data is persisted. In some cases, the validation error is raised after the data has already been committed, which allows restricted GL or Cost Center values to be saved.

We explored several options but faced limitations:

  • BAdI MM_PUR_S4_SES_CHECK_ITEM
    This BAdI provides header and item data but does not expose account assignment data (SESACCOUNTASSIGNMENT) where the GL and Cost Center information is stored.

  • BAdI FAGL_SEGMENT_BS_ACC
    This is triggered during FI posting, but not all GL accounts trigger it, making it unreliable for SES validation.

We also attempted the following:

  • Validation during SES creation, copy, and change scenarios

  • Reading CDS views

  • Calling FI_CHECK_ACCOUNT_ASSIGNMENT for GL + Cost Center validation

  • Extending account assignment data via the Custom Logic app

However, the available enhancement points in the SES flow do not consistently execute before the save, making it difficult to reliably block invalid combinations. Additionally, some relevant BAdIs appear to be SAP-internal and not open for customer implementation.

Our current framework version also does not expose the GL or Cost Center parameters that seem to exist in newer SES versions.

Has anyone successfully implemented a reliable validation or restriction for GL Account and Cost Center combinations in the Fiori SES app before saving?

Any guidance or recommended enhancement point would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Do SAP transformation teams usually include change management roles?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

While reading some project reports we noticed that some SAP transformation teams include roles like Change Management Consultant or Communications Specialist to help with adoption, but it doesn’t seem to be consistent across projects. In your experience, are these roles usually part of the team, or are they often overlooked?

entry.title: Master Data Material Best Practice
entry.content:
Hi,

I need a suggestion for assigning descriptions to materials in SAP (MAKT-MAKTX). The problem we have is that users request the creation of descriptions by assigning names of their own choosing, and this varies greatly, resulting in duplicates because they reverse the names. For example:

  • 4000027786 - VARIADOR SINAMIC V20 6SL3210-5BE22-2UV0
  • 4000027745  - VARIADOR V20 6SL3210-5BE22-2UV0 SINAMIC 

They are the same but were created with different codes
